codeReview.summary: The API documentation implementation is comprehensive and well-structured. The worker successfully added @elysiajs/swagger plugin to the API, configured it with proper metadata (title, version, description, tags), and added detailed OpenAPI annotations to inventory, automation, and staff routes. Each annotated endpoint includes summary, description, security requirements (bearerAuth), and response schemas. The documentation covers 20+ endpoints across the three main domains.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The OpenAPI annotations follow a consistent pattern using Elysia's detail property with tags, summary, description, security, and responses. This pattern should be documented in AGENTS.md for future API documentation work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: packages/api/src/api/routes/inventory.ts:32-46 - Example of detailed OpenAPI annotation pattern used consistently across routes

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker correctly used Spanish language for all user-facing documentation (endpoint summaries and descriptions) as specified in the project guidelines, while keeping code comments in English.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: packages/api/src/api/routes/inventory.ts:35 - summary: 'Listar productos', description: 'Obtiene una lista de productos...'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The swagger plugin is configured at /api/docs path and includes comprehensive tag organization (Inventory, Automation, Staff, Auth, Profiles, Clients, Services, Reservations). This configuration should be noted for developers needing to access API documentation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: packages/api/src/index.ts:85-120 - Swagger configuration with documentation info and tags

summary: Feature m9-2-api-documentation successfully implemented OpenAPI/Swagger documentation for the CitaBot API. The implementation includes: (1) @elysiajs/swagger plugin installation and configuration, (2) Detailed annotations for inventory endpoints (products CRUD, stock adjustment, movements, suppliers, purchase orders), (3) Detailed annotations for automation endpoints (templates, CRUD, triggers, actions, execution logs), (4) Detailed annotations for staff endpoints (CRUD, service assignments, availability). All annotations include proper tags, summaries in Spanish, descriptions, security requirements (bearerAuth), and response schemas. The code compiles without errors in the modified files.
